english: public; to show something to others; Tar. 24, 1 should be understood: to make clear or manifest to one's self, to perceive, know, understand; mṅón-du byín-pa (mngon du byin pa) to disclose, reveal (secrets, the future) Glr.; to make known (one's wishes) Glr.; mṅón-du gyúr-ba (mngon du gyur ba) to be revealed or disclosed, to make one's appearance, raṅ-byúṅ ye-śés mṅón-du gyúr-pas (rang byung ye shes mngon du gyur pas) as the self-originated wisdom has revealed itself to us Mil. - mṅón-par (mngon par) adv. manifestly, openly, evidently; often = entirely, highly, greatly, very, mṅón-par rdzogs (mngon par rdzogs) Was. (246) complete fulfilment; in the sense of 'very' it may also be taken in mṅón-par dgao (mngon par dga'o), in the legends of Buddha, 'they rejoiced very much', though also one of the other significations of abhinanda might help to explain these words. Comp. mṅón-(par) brjód(-pa) (mngon par brjod pa) = abhidāna, a collection of synonyms, of which some are mentioned in Burn. I. and II. - mṅón-rtágs (mngon rtags) proof, argument; sign or token of the truth of a thing Dzl. 72, 2. - mṅón-(par) rtógs(-pa) (mngon par rtogs pa) 1. a clear comprehension Was. (287). 2. a hymnlike description of a Lha from top to toe, v. also Schl. 260. - mṅón-mtó (mngon mtho) re-birth as Lha or as man Thgy. (Schr.); also n. of a religion in Paradise. - mṅón-pa-pa (mngon pa pa) an Abhidharma scholar. - mṅón-spyód (mngon spyod) Sch. cruelty, severity; Schf. more corr.: witchcraft, Ssk. abhicāra, Pth., drág-po mṅón-spyód-kyi las (drag po mngon spyod kyi las) Tar. frq. - mṅón-mtsán (mngon mtshan) Lex. w.e., Sch.; an evident sign. - mṅón-(par) śés(-pa) (mngon par shes pa), resp. mk'yén(-pa) (mkhyen pa), Ssk. abhijñā, a kind of clairvoyance, gift of supernatural perception, of which five species are enumerated, viz. assuming any form at will; seeing and hearing to any distance, knowing a man's thoughts, knowing a man's condition and antecedents; originally used as a vb.: to be clear-seeing Pth. - mṅón-súm-du (mngon sum du) 1. openly, publicly Dzl.; more frq. 2. bodily, personally; like dṅós-su (dngos su), e.g. to appear, to instruct, in person (Tar.); to know by one's own personal experience (W.).

I. kettle-drum, drum, K'ár-rṅa (mkhar rnga) v. K'ár (mkhar); rdzá-rṅa (rdza rnga) Glr., Cs.: 'a drum of earthenware'; rgyál-rṅa (rgyal rnga) the beating of drums after a victory, bág-rṅa (bag rnga) at nuptial festivities, *lhá-ṅa* (lha nga) Ld. for the king; *źéṅ-ṅa* (zheng nga) and *zím-ṅa* (zim nga) Ld. a morning and evening serenade with an accompaniment of drums; k'ríms-kyi rṅá-bo che brdúṅs-te (khrims kyi rnga bo che brdungs te) the beat or sound of the large proclamation drum (prop. law-drum) Glr. - Comp. rṅa-sgrá (rnga sgra) 1. sound of the drum, or kettle-drum 2. n. of a Buddha, = dón-yód-grub-pa (don yod grub pa) or Amoghasiddha. - *ṅa-lcág* (nga lcag) W., rṅa-rdég (rnga rdeg) Sch., rṅa-dbyúg (rnga dbyug) Cs., rṅa-yáb (rnga yab) Sch. drum-stick. - rṅá-pa (rnga pa) a drummer Cs., rṅa-dpón (rnga dpon) a chief drummer. - rṅa-lpágs (rnga lpags) drum-skin. - rṅa-yú (rnga yu) handle of a kettle-drum (the larger kettle-drums being held up during the play by means of a handle or stick). - rṅa-śíṅ (rnga shing) the wooden body of a drum Cs. - rṅa-śón (rnga shon) kettle-drum music Sch. - rṅa-gsáṅs (rnga gsangs) (also rṅa-bsáṅs (rnga bsangs)) a loud beat or roll of the kettle-drum Sch. II. for rṅa-móṅ (rnga mong), and rṅá-ma (rnga ma).

pf. brṅas (brngas), fut. brṅa (brnga), imp. rṅos (rngos), to mow, to cut, to reap, ,bru ('bru), or lo-tóg zór-bas (lo tog zor bas) to cut the harvest with a sickle; brtsás-ma brṅás-pa (brtsas ma brngas pa) the reaped corn; rṅá-mk'an (rnga mkhan) the mower, reaper.

W., *ṅa-móṅ* (nga mong) C., camel, rṅa-gséb (rnga gseb) male camel, rṅá-mo (rnga mo) female camel; a camel in general; rṅa-p'rúg (rnga phrug) the young of a camel; rṅa-rgód (rnga rgod) a wild camel; rṅa-bál (rnga bal) camel's hair.

1. tail. 2. in a special sense: yak's tail Mil. - rṅá-ma gyúg-pa (rnga ma g.yug pa) Sch., *ṅá-ma tóg-ce* (nga ma tog ce) (lit. skrog-pa (skrog pa)) W., to wag the tail. - rṅa-yáb (rnga yab) 1. a yak's tail, used for fanning and dusting. 2. rṅa-yáb (rnga yab), and rṅa-yab-gźán (rnga yab gzhan), n. of two fabulous islands in the south of Asia Cs.
Lex., C. and W., contempt, disdain; *ṅan-čén jhé-pa* (ngan chen jhe pa) C., *čó-ce* (co ce) W. to despise, contemn.
I. sbst. 1. reward, fee, hire, wages; rṅán-pa máṅ-po (rngan pa mang po) the wages are high; rṅán-pa sbyín-pa (rngan pa sbyin pa) or gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) to
